bullets.content: IF the user requests any order-related action (exchange, return, modify, or cancel) → THEN request identification by asking for either (1) first name + last name + zip code OR (2) email address before doing anything else
IF identification is provided as name + zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip) and then call get_user_details(user_id)
IF identification is provided as email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email) and then call get_user_details(user_id)

IF user details are retrieved → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for the user's orders (or the specific orders referenced) to determine item_ids, item options, prices, and order status before taking action

IF the user requests an exchange or wants details about replacement variants → THEN call get_product_details(product_id) to obtain variant item_ids, availability, prices, and option attributes (size, switch type, backlight, compatibility, color, etc.)
IF the user references multiple orders or is ambiguous about which order contains the item → THEN list or retrieve the relevant orders and their item_ids to confirm which order/item to act on
IF order.status == "delivered" AND the user requests an exchange of delivered items → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
IF order.status == "delivered" AND the user requests a return of delivered items → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)

IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests to change (modify) a pending item (e.g., size or variant) → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests cancellation → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason)
IF executing any exchange/return/modify/cancel that affects payment → THEN include the appropriate payment_method_id (the original payment method on record for that order) in the tool call

IF performing an exchange and replacement variants have price differences → THEN compute/obtain the exchange price difference (via product details) and include payment_method_id so the system can charge or refund the difference in the exchange tool call

IF replacement variant availability must be verified → THEN check get_product_details and only use new_item_ids that are available (set new_item_ids accordingly when calling exchange_delivered_order_items or modify_pending_order_items)

IF multiple variant attributes or trade-offs exist (e.g., size vs backlight vs switch type) → THEN ask the user which specific attributes they prefer (explicit questions such as "What backlight would you like? White or no backlight?")

IF the user has selected replacement/return/cancellation options → THEN explicitly ask for confirmation (e.g., "Just to confirm you want those two changes yes?") before executing the corresponding tool call

IF an action (exchange/return/modify/cancel) completes successfully → THEN notify the user that the action was completed (e.g., "Ok I made the change", "Done. Both orders have been cancelled") and ask if they need anything else

IF a requested action cannot be performed due to policy or system constraints → THEN explain the constraint and provide the allowed alternative(s) (e.g., explain refunds can only go to the original payment method or an existing gift card)
IF no valid identification (name+zip or email) has been provided → THEN do not perform order lookups or action tool calls and request identification first

IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the original payment method used for that order → THEN refuse the request and state the policy: refunds can only be issued to the original payment method or an existing gift card

IF the exact requested replacement variant does not exist/available → THEN present available alternatives and ask the user to prioritize attributes (the agent may propose trade-offs such as keeping size but changing backlight or vice versa)
IF order.status == "delivered" → THEN do not use pending-order modification/cancellation tools (use delivered-order exchange/return tools instead)
IF order.status == "pending" → THEN do not use delivered-order exchange/return tools (use pending-order modification or cancel tools instead)
IF the user requests cancellation of a pending order → THEN ask for the reason for cancellation before calling cancel_pending_order

  [info-003] IF identification is provided as email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email) and then call get_user_details(user_id)
  [info-004] IF user details are retrieved → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for the user's orders (or the specific orders referenced) to determine item_ids, item options, prices, and order status before taking action
  [info-005] IF the user requests an exchange or wants details about replacement variants → THEN call get_product_details(product_id) to obtain variant item_ids, availability, prices, and option attributes (size, switch type, backlight, compatibility, color, etc.)

## Action Execution

  [acti-002] IF order.status == "delivered" AND the user requests a return of delivered items → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-003] IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests to change (modify) a pending item (e.g., size or variant) → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)

  [acti-005] IF executing any exchange/return/modify/cancel that affects payment → THEN include the appropriate payment_method_id (the original payment method on record for that order) in the tool call
  [acti-006] IF performing an exchange and replacement variants have price differences → THEN compute/obtain the exchange price difference (via product details) and include payment_method_id so the system can charge or refund the difference in the exchange tool call
  [acti-007] IF replacement variant availability must be verified → THEN check get_product_details and only use new_item_ids that are available (set new_item_ids accordingly when calling exchange_delivered_order_items or modify_pending_order_items)

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF multiple variant attributes or trade-offs exist (e.g., size vs backlight vs switch type) → THEN ask the user which specific attributes they prefer (explicit questions such as "What backlight would you like? White or no backlight?")
  [conf-002] IF the user has selected replacement/return/cancellation options → THEN explicitly ask for confirmation (e.g., "Just to confirm you want those two changes yes?") before executing the corresponding tool call
  [conf-003] IF an action (exchange/return/modify/cancel) completes successfully → THEN notify the user that the action was completed (e.g., "Ok I made the change", "Done. Both orders have been cancelled") and ask if they need anything else
  [conf-004] IF a requested action cannot be performed due to policy or system constraints → THEN explain the constraint and provide the allowed alternative(s) (e.g., explain refunds can only go to the original payment method or an existing gift card)

  [prec-002] IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the original payment method used for that order → THEN refuse the request and state the policy: refunds can only be issued to the original payment method or an existing gift card
  [prec-003] IF the exact requested replacement variant does not exist/available → THEN present available alternatives and ask the user to prioritize attributes (the agent may propose trade-offs such as keeping size but changing backlight or vice versa)
  [prec-004] IF order.status == "delivered" → THEN do not use pending-order modification/cancellation tools (use delivered-order exchange/return tools instead)
  [prec-005] IF order.status == "pending" → THEN do not use delivered-order exchange/return tools (use pending-order modification or cancel tools instead)
  [prec-006] IF the user requests cancellation of a pending order → THEN ask for the reason for cancellation before calling cancel_pending_order

deltas.content: IF items in the same order are requested to be acted on → THEN do not perform both a return and an exchange simultaneously on that single order; require the user to choose one action (return OR exchange) for that order before proceeding.
deltas.rationale: In task 19 the agent refused to do both a return and exchanges on items that belonged to the same order and had the user choose one action, indicating the system/process does not allow mixing return+exchange on the same order.

deltas.content: IF executing any exchange/return/modify/cancel that affects payment → THEN include the appropriate payment_method_id in the tool call. By default, refunds must be issued to the original payment method on record for that order (or an existing gift card). If the action requires charging an additional amount (e.g., exchanging to a higher-priced item or modifying to a more expensive variant), the system should use the original payment method unless the user explicitly authorizes a different payment method; record that explicit authorization before using a non-original payment method for charges.
deltas.rationale: In task 52 the exchange used a different payment method (PayPal) than the original payment method on file; the playbook should allow using a different payment method for charging extra with explicit user authorization, while preserving the rule that refunds go to the original method.

deltas.content: IF an order/item has already had a modification, exchange, or return applied once → THEN do not allow additional modifications/exchanges/returns to that same order/item (actions are limited to a single change); require the agent to inform the user of this limitation before taking action.
deltas.rationale: Agents in tasks 19, 41, and 46 repeatedly warned that certain actions (returns/exchanges/modifications) can only be done once; this indicates a system constraint that must be enforced and communicated.

deltas.content: IF the user has selected replacement/return/cancellation/modification options → THEN explicitly ask for confirmation before executing the corresponding tool call (e.g., "Just to confirm you want those two changes yes?"). If the action is irreversible or limited (e.g., only one modification/exchange/return allowed), the confirmation prompt must explicitly warn the user of the limitation/irreversibility.
deltas.rationale: Agents in tasks 19, 41, and 46 gave explicit warnings that actions are one-time or irreversible before proceeding; the confirmation rule should require such warnings when applicable.

deltas.content: IF the user requests to update their account address or the shipping address for their orders → THEN request identification by asking for either (1) first name + last name + zip code OR (2) email address before doing anything else; after identification, call find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) or find_user_id_by_email(...) and then call get_user_details(user_id).
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents always request identification before making account or order address changes (e.g., Conversation 1) but the playbook lacks an explicit rule for address updates.

deltas.content: IF the user requests an account address update → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, new_address). IF the user requests order shipping address updates → THEN retrieve each referenced order with get_order_details(order_id) and: for orders with status == "pending" call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, new_address); for orders with status == "delivered" or "processed" do NOT modify the order address and inform the user that those orders cannot be changed (offer alternatives such as returning/exchanging after delivery).
deltas.rationale: Conversation 1 shows agents updating account address and pending-order addresses while refusing to modify processed orders; the playbook has no explicit address-update action steps.

deltas.content: IF order.status == "delivered" OR order.status == "processed" → THEN do not use pending-order modification/cancellation tools (use delivered-order exchange/return tools instead); only orders with status "pending" may be modified/cancelled via pending-order tools.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories treat "processed" orders as non-modifiable (Conversation 1), so the delivered-only rule should be broadened to include "processed".

deltas.content: IF executing any exchange/return/modify/cancel that affects payment → THEN ask the user which payment method they want to use (present available methods on file). If the user responds 'use the same method as before' or equivalent, use the original payment method on record for that order. Do not proceed with the action until the user explicitly confirms the payment method.
deltas.rationale: Agents consistently asked users to confirm which payment method to use (or accepted 'same as before') before performing actions that affect payment; the playbook required including a payment_method_id but did not require confirming it with the user first.

deltas.content: IF the chosen payment method does not have sufficient funds or cannot cover a required price difference for an exchange/modify/return → THEN inform the user that the payment method is insufficient, present options to (a) provide or add an alternate payment method, (b) split or cover the difference with another available method if supported, or (c) cancel/hold the change. If the user has no alternate methods on file and cannot add one, offer to transfer to a human agent. Do not execute the action tool call until a valid payment method that can cover any required charge is provided.
deltas.rationale: An attempted exchange failed due to insufficient gift card balance; the agent needed to stop, inform the user, and offer alternatives (add payment method or transfer to human). The playbook lacked guidance for insufficient-payment scenarios.

deltas.content: IF the user has selected replacement/return/cancellation options → THEN explicitly ask for confirmation (e.g., "Just to confirm you want those two changes, yes?") before executing the corresponding tool call. If the action is irreversible or subject to a system limit (for example, only one modification allowed per order), explicitly notify the user of that constraint during confirmation and require the user to acknowledge they understand before proceeding.
deltas.rationale: Agents warned users about one-time modification limits and sought explicit acknowledgment before proceeding; conf-002 required confirmation but did not require communicating irreversible/system limits as part of that confirmation.

deltas.content: IF executing any exchange/return/modify/cancel that affects payment → THEN include an appropriate payment_method_id in the tool call. Use the original payment method by default; however, if the customer explicitly requests a different payment method that is on their account (for example, an on-account gift card or another stored payment method) and that choice complies with refund/payment policy (refunds still must go to the original payment method or an existing gift card), use the customer-requested payment method and include its payment_method_id in the tool call.
deltas.rationale: Agents in the trajectories used a gift card (not the original card) to pay an exchange price difference when the customer explicitly requested it. The playbook must allow using a customer-selected on-account payment method (e.g., gift card) while preserving refund constraints.

deltas.content: IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests to update the shipping address for that pending order → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the order's shipping address and confirm the change with the user.
deltas.rationale: A trajectory required changing the shipping address on a pending order and the agent used a modify_pending_order_address tool call. The playbook lacked an explicit rule for updating addresses on pending orders.

deltas.content: IF the user requests to update their account default shipping address → THEN (after verifying identity) call update_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the user's default address on file and confirm the update with the user.
deltas.rationale: A user asked to change their account/default address in a trajectory; the playbook should explicitly include a standard action for updating a user's default shipping address when requested.

deltas.content: IF executing any exchange/return/modify/cancel that affects payment → THEN include the appropriate payment_method_id in the tool call. By default use the original payment method on record for that order. Exception: for pending-order modifications where the user explicitly requests an alternate payment method to cover any price difference or re-charge, the agent may use the user-specified payment method (include it in the tool call). Refunds issued as part of returns/exchanges must follow refund rules (refunds can only go to the original payment method or an existing gift card).

deltas.content: IF the user has selected replacement/return/cancellation/modification/address-change options that will alter the order → THEN explicitly ask for confirmation (e.g., "Just to confirm you want those changes to this order, yes?") before executing the corresponding tool call, unless the user has already explicitly and unambiguously asked the agent to proceed.

deltas.content: IF order.status == "cancelled" → THEN the cancellation cannot be reversed (orders cannot be reinstated). deltas.rationale: Trajectory evidence shows agents verifying a gift card balance and informing the customer of the remaining balance before charging it; playbook lacked an explicit rule to check gift card balance, show resulting balance, and get confirmation when using gift cards for charges.

  [info-004] IF user details are retrieved → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for the user's orders (or the specific orders referenced) to determine item_ids, item options, prices, and order status before taking action
  [info-005] IF the user requests an exchange or wants details about replacement variants → THEN call get_product_details(product_id) to obtain variant item_ids, availability, prices, and option attributes (size, switch type, backlight, compatibility, color, etc.)
  [info-006] IF the user references multiple orders or is ambiguous about which order contains the item → THEN list or retrieve the relevant orders and their item_ids to confirm which order/item to act on

## Action Execution
  [acti-001] IF order.status == "delivered" AND the user requests an exchange of delivered items → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-002] IF order.status == "delivered" AND the user requests a return of delivered items → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-003] IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests to change (modify) a pending item (e.g., size or variant) → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-004] IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests cancellation → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason)
  [acti-005] IF executing any exchange/return/modify/cancel that affects payment → THEN include the appropriate payment_method_id (the original payment method on record for that order) in the tool call
  [acti-006] IF performing an exchange and replacement variants have price differences → THEN compute/obtain the exchange price difference (via product details) and include payment_method_id so the system can charge or refund the difference in the exchange tool call
  [acti-007] IF replacement variant availability must be verified → THEN check get_product_details and only use new_item_ids that are available (set new_item_ids accordingly when calling exchange_delivered_order_items or modify_pending_order_items)

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF multiple variant attributes or trade-offs exist (e.g., size vs backlight vs switch type) → THEN ask the user which specific attributes they prefer (explicit questions such as "What backlight would you like? White or no backlight?")
  [conf-002] IF the user has selected replacement/return/cancellation options → THEN explicitly ask for confirmation (e.g., "Just to confirm you want those two changes yes?") before executing the corresponding tool call
  [conf-003] IF an action (exchange/return/modify/cancel) completes successfully → THEN notify the user that the action was completed (e.g., "Ok I made the change", "Done. Both orders have been cancelled") and ask if they need anything else
  [conf-004] IF a requested action cannot be performed due to policy or system constraints → THEN explain the constraint and provide the allowed alternative(s) (e.g., explain refunds can only go to the original payment method or an existing gift card)

  [prec-002] IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the original payment method used for that order → THEN refuse the request and state the policy: refunds can only be issued to the original payment method or an existing gift card
  [prec-003] IF the exact requested replacement variant does not exist/available → THEN present available alternatives and ask the user to prioritize attributes (the agent may propose trade-offs such as keeping size but changing backlight or vice versa)
  [prec-004] IF order.status == "delivered" → THEN do not use pending-order modification/cancellation tools (use delivered-order exchange/return tools instead)
  [prec-005] IF order.status == "pending" → THEN do not use delivered-order exchange/return tools (use pending-order modification or cancel tools instead)
  [prec-006] IF the user requests cancellation of a pending order → THEN ask for the reason for cancellation before calling cancel_pending_order

deltas.content: IF executing any exchange/return/modify/cancel that affects payment → THEN include a payment_method_id in the tool call. By default, use the original payment method on record for that order. If the user explicitly requests and confirms using a different on-file payment method (for example, another card or an on-file gift card) for an exchange price difference or other payment adjustment, the agent may use that on-file payment method instead. Do not accept external or unlisted payment methods.
deltas.rationale: Agents in the trajectories used an alternate on-file card for exchange-related payment adjustments when the user explicitly requested it; the rule should allow this while preventing refunds to external/unlisted methods.

deltas.content: IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the original payment method used for that order → THEN inform the user that refunds are generally issued to the original payment method or an existing gift card. Refuse requests to refund to an external or unlisted payment method. However, if the user explicitly requests and confirms using a different on-file payment method for an exchange price difference or other payment adjustment, the agent may proceed using that on-file method.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show users requesting refunds/adjustments to alternate on-file cards and agents honoring those requests after confirmation; update the policy to reflect allowed use of alternate on-file payment methods while preserving the core restriction against external/unlisted methods.

deltas.content: IF the user requests any modification to a pending order (such as changing shipping address or modifying pending items) → THEN explicitly ask the user to confirm the requested changes (e.g., "Just to confirm, you want me to change the shipping address for these pending orders to X, right?") before executing modify_pending_order_address or modify_pending_order_items tool calls.
deltas.rationale: Agents asked for explicit confirmation before modifying pending-order addresses in the trajectories; the playbook should require confirmation for pending-order modifications in addition to returns/exchanges/cancellations.

deltas.content: IF the user requests to change their account's default shipping address → THEN verify the user's identity (if not already done) and call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the stored default address. Before making the change, confirm with the user that they want the address set permanently as their default.
deltas.rationale: An agent updated a user's default address via modify_user_address after verifying identity (Conversation 4). The playbook lacked explicit guidance for changing stored user addresses and confirming the permanent nature of the change.

deltas.rationale: Agents in multiple trajectories applied user-specified payment methods to pay or receive exchange/modify differences (e.g., charging a gift card or a different card than the original). deltas.content: IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the original payment method used for that order → THEN refuse the request and state the policy: refunds (money returned to the customer) can only be issued to the original payment method on record for that order or to an existing store/gift card. Note: this rule does not prevent the customer from specifying a different valid payment method to be used to pay any additional charges for an exchange or modification; it only governs where net refunds are issued.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories demonstrate agents accepting user-specified methods to pay charge differences while still needing to enforce that refunds go to the original method or a gift card. The original rule should be clarified to distinguish refunds from user-requested payment methods for charges.

deltas.content: IF the user asks for available product options, asks for the "cheapest" model, or does not provide a product_id → THEN call list_all_product_types() to map product names to product_ids as needed, and then call get_product_details(product_id) to retrieve variant item_ids, availability, and prices; compute and present the lowest-priced available variant (or list alternatives) to the user.
deltas.rationale: Agents invoked list_all_product_types() before fetching product details and computing the cheapest variant (e.g., task 109) — the playbook doesn't include guidance to list product types or compute/present the cheapest available variant.

deltas.content: IF the system enforces a limit of one exchange per order (or similar item-exchange limits) → THEN inform the user of this constraint before initiating exchanges and ask the user to confirm which single item(s) to exchange or to prioritize items.
deltas.rationale: An agent informed the user that only one exchange is possible and asked which item to prioritize before proceeding (task 8). The playbook should capture this system constraint and require agents to notify the user and ask for prioritization.

deltas.content: IF an exchange/return/modify/cancel will affect payment AND the user has multiple payment methods on file OR has not specified which to use → THEN ask the user which payment method they prefer (or confirm using the original payment method on the order) before executing the action, and include the chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
deltas.rationale: Agents asked users to confirm which payment method to use when price differences or refunds were possible (e.g., task 109); while the playbook requires including payment_method_id, it lacks an explicit rule to prompt the user when multiple methods exist or preference is unspecified.

iterations.policy:
## Information Gathering
  [info-001] IF the user requests any order-related action (exchange, return, modify, or cancel) → THEN request identification by asking for either (1) first name + last name + zip code OR (2) email address before doing anything else
  [info-002] IF identification is provided as name + zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip) and then call get_user_details(user_id)
  [info-003] IF identification is provided as email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email) and then call get_user_details(user_id)
  [info-004] IF user details are retrieved → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for the user's orders (or the specific orders referenced) to determine item_ids, item options, prices, and order status before taking action
  [info-005] IF the user requests an exchange or wants details about replacement variants → THEN call get_product_details(product_id) to obtain variant item_ids, availability, prices, and option attributes (size, switch type, backlight, compatibility, color, etc.)
  [info-006] IF the user references multiple orders or is ambiguous about which order contains the item → THEN list or retrieve the relevant orders and their item_ids to confirm which order/item to act on

## Action Execution
  [acti-001] IF order.status == "delivered" AND the user requests an exchange of delivered items → THEN call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-002] IF order.status == "delivered" AND the user requests a return of delivered items → THEN call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-003] IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests to change (modify) a pending item (e.g., size or variant) → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
  [acti-004] IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests cancellation → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason)
  [acti-005] IF executing any exchange/return/modify/cancel that affects payment → THEN include the appropriate payment_method_id (the original payment method on record for that order) in the tool call
  [acti-006] IF performing an exchange and replacement variants have price differences → THEN compute/obtain the exchange price difference (via product details) and include payment_method_id so the system can charge or refund the difference in the exchange tool call
  [acti-007] IF replacement variant availability must be verified → THEN check get_product_details and only use new_item_ids that are available (set new_item_ids accordingly when calling exchange_delivered_order_items or modify_pending_order_items)

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF multiple variant attributes or trade-offs exist (e.g., size vs backlight vs switch type) → THEN ask the user which specific attributes they prefer (explicit questions such as "What backlight would you like? White or no backlight?")
  [conf-002] IF the user has selected replacement/return/cancellation options → THEN explicitly ask for confirmation (e.g., "Just to confirm you want those two changes yes?") before executing the corresponding tool call
  [conf-003] IF an action (exchange/return/modify/cancel) completes successfully → THEN notify the user that the action was completed (e.g., "Ok I made the change", "Done. Both orders have been cancelled") and ask if they need anything else
  [conf-004] IF a requested action cannot be performed due to policy or system constraints → THEN explain the constraint and provide the allowed alternative(s) (e.g., explain refunds can only go to the original payment method or an existing gift card)

## Precondition Rules
  [prec-001] IF no valid identification (name+zip or email) has been provided → THEN do not perform order lookups or action tool calls and request identification first
  [prec-002] IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the original payment method used for that order → THEN refuse the request and state the policy: refunds can only be issued to the original payment method or an existing gift card
  [prec-003] IF the exact requested replacement variant does not exist/available → THEN present available alternatives and ask the user to prioritize attributes (the agent may propose trade-offs such as keeping size but changing backlight or vice versa)
  [prec-004] IF order.status == "delivered" → THEN do not use pending-order modification/cancellation tools (use delivered-order exchange/return tools instead)
  [prec-005] IF order.status == "pending" → THEN do not use delivered-order exchange/return tools (use pending-order modification or cancel tools instead)
  [prec-006] IF the user requests cancellation of a pending order → THEN ask for the reason for cancellation before calling cancel_pending_order

deltas.content: IF the agent needs to identify a product or find a product_id and the user doesn't provide it → THEN call list_all_product_types() to discover the product_id(s), then call get_product_details(product_id) for the chosen product.
deltas.rationale: Agents used list_all_product_types() to find product IDs before calling get_product_details when users didn't supply product IDs (seen in exchanges/modifications).

deltas.content: IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests to change the shipping address for that pending order → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) (include any required validation) and treat address changes as a pending-order modification.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents modifying the shipping address of pending orders via modify_pending_order_address; the playbook lacked an explicit rule for address modifications.

deltas.content: IF the user has selected replacement/return/modify/cancel/address-change options → THEN explicitly ask for confirmation (e.g., "Just to confirm you want those two changes yes?") before executing the corresponding tool call.
deltas.rationale: Evidence shows confirmations are expected not only for exchanges/returns/cancellations but also for item modifications and address changes; conf-002 should explicitly include modifications and address changes.

bullets.content: IF the user requests any order-related action (exchange, return, modify, or cancel) OR any payment-related action connected to an order (e.g., split payment attempts, changing payment method for an order, or other payment alterations) → THEN request identification by asking for either (1) first name + last name + zip code OR (2) email address before doing anything else
IF identification is provided as name + zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip) and then call get_user_details(user_id)
IF identification is provided as email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email) and then call get_user_details(user_id)

IF user details are retrieved → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for the user's orders (or the specific orders referenced) to determine item_ids, item options, prices, and order status before taking action

IF the user requests an exchange or wants details about replacement variants → THEN call get_product_details(product_id) to obtain variant item_ids, availability, prices, and option attributes (size, switch type, backlight, compatibility, color, etc.)
IF the user references multiple orders or is ambiguous about which order contains the item → THEN list or retrieve the relevant orders and their item_ids to confirm which order/item to act on

IF order.status == "delivered" AND the user requests an exchange of delivered items → call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id). IF order.status == "delivered" AND the user requests a return of delivered items → call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id)

IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests to change (modify) a pending item (e.g., size or variant) → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id)
IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests cancellation → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason)

IF executing any exchange/return/modify/cancel that affects payment → THEN include an appropriate payment_method_id in the tool call. Use the original payment method on the order by default. If the user explicitly requests a different valid payment method (for example: a different credit/debit card, PayPal, or an existing gift card) to be used to pay any additional charge or to accept an issued credit for an exchange/modify, use that user-specified method in the tool call. However, do not accept requests to issue refunds to an unrelated external payment method; refunds must follow the refund policy (see precondition rules).

IF performing an exchange and replacement variants have price differences → THEN compute/obtain the exchange price difference (via product details) and include payment_method_id so the system can charge or refund the difference in the exchange tool call. If the customer elects to use a gift card to cover any additional charge, allow the gift_card payment_method_id to be used (and verify the gift card balance before finalizing). If the difference results in a refund, refund to the original payment method or to an existing gift card only (per refund policy).

IF replacement variant availability must be verified → THEN check get_product_details and only use new_item_ids that are available (set new_item_ids accordingly when calling exchange_delivered_order_items or modify_pending_order_items)

IF multiple variant attributes or trade-offs exist (e.g., size vs backlight vs switch type) → THEN ask the user which specific attributes they prefer (explicit questions such as "What backlight would you like? White or no backlight?")

IF the user has selected replacement/return/modify/cancel/address-change options → THEN explicitly ask for confirmation (e.g., "Just to confirm you want those two changes yes?") before executing the corresponding tool call.

IF an action (exchange/return/modify/cancel) completes successfully → THEN notify the user that the action was completed (e.g., "Ok I made the change", "Done. Both orders have been cancelled") and ask if they need anything else

IF a requested action cannot be performed due to policy or system constraints → THEN explain the constraint and provide the allowed alternative(s) (e.g., explain refunds can only go to the original payment method or an existing gift card)
IF no valid identification (name+zip or email) has been provided → THEN do not perform order lookups or action tool calls and request identification first

IF the user requests a refund to a different payment method than the original payment method used for that order → THEN refuse the request and state the policy: refunds (money returned to the customer) can only be issued to the original payment method on record for that order or to an existing store/gift card. Note: this rule does not prevent the customer from specifying a different valid payment method to be used to pay any additional charges for an exchange or modification; it only governs where net refunds are issued.

IF the exact requested replacement variant does not exist/available → THEN present available alternatives and ask the user to prioritize attributes (the agent may propose trade-offs such as keeping size but changing backlight or vice versa)

IF order.status == "pending" → THEN use pending-order modification/cancellation tools and do not use delivered-order exchange/return tools; IF order.status == "delivered" OR order.status == "processed" → THEN use delivered-order exchange/return tools and do not use pending-order modification/cancellation tools.

IF the user requests cancellation of a pending order → THEN obtain a reason for cancellation before calling cancel_pending_order. If the user has already provided a clear reason earlier in the conversation (e.g., in their initial request), use that stated reason and proceed without asking again.

IF items in the same order are requested to be acted on → THEN do not perform both a return and an exchange simultaneously on that single order; require the user to choose one action (return OR exchange) for that order before proceeding.

IF an order/item has already had a modification, exchange, or return applied once → THEN do not allow additional modifications/exchanges/returns to that same order/item (actions are limited to a single change); require the agent to inform the user of this limitation before taking action.

IF the user requests to update their account address or the shipping address for their orders → THEN request identification by asking for either (1) first name + last name + zip code OR (2) email address before doing anything else; after identification, call find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) or find_user_id_by_email(...) and then call get_user_details(user_id).

IF the user requests an account address update → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, new_address). IF the user requests order shipping address updates → THEN for each referenced order call get_order_details(order_id) to determine status; if status == "pending" call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, country, state, zip) to update the shipping address and then call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm the update; if status == "delivered" or status == "processed" do NOT modify the order address and inform the user that those orders cannot be changed, offering alternatives such as returning or exchanging after delivery.

IF executing any exchange/return/modify/cancel that affects payment → THEN ask the user which payment method they want to use (present available methods on file). If the user responds 'use the same method as before' or equivalent, use the original payment method on record for that order. Do not proceed with the action until the user explicitly confirms the payment method.

IF the chosen payment method does not have sufficient funds or cannot cover a required price difference for an exchange/modify/return → THEN inform the user that the payment method is insufficient, present options to (a) provide or add an alternate payment method, (b) split or cover the difference with another available method if supported, or (c) cancel/hold the change. If the user has no alternate methods on file and cannot add one, offer to transfer to a human agent. Do not execute the action tool call until a valid payment method that can cover any required charge is provided.

IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests to change or update the shipping address for that pending order → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) (including any required validation) to update the shipping address for that specific pending order, treat the address change as a pending-order modification, and confirm the updated address with the user and inform them of any order total/price impact.

IF the user requests to change their account default shipping address → THEN verify the user's identity (if not already verified), confirm with the user that they want the provided address set permanently as their default, call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the stored default address, and confirm to the user that the default address has been updated.

IF order.status == "cancelled" → THEN the cancellation cannot be reversed (orders cannot be reinstated). Inform the user that the original order cannot be undone and offer alternatives: place a new order for the same items, check current availability, or transfer/escalate to human agents if they request further review.

IF order.status == "pending" AND the user requests cancellation of one or more items within that pending order → THEN inform the user that individual-item or partial cancellations are not supported for pending orders and that the entire pending order must be canceled; present alternatives (cancel the entire order — if chosen, ask for the cancellation reason before proceeding; modify item variants if possible; place a new order with the desired items; or leave the order as-is and, if items ship, return delivered items after receipt) and ask which alternative they'd prefer.

IF the user requests to split payment across multiple payment methods for a single order → THEN refuse the request and state the policy: only one payment method can be used per order; offer allowed alternatives (use a single payment method, cancel and place separate orders, or pay the balance via another full order).

IF the user asks for totals or aggregate spending across their past purchases (e.g., total spent on a product type) → THEN retrieve the user's relevant orders with get_order_details, identify matching items across those orders, compute the requested aggregate (using a calculation tool if needed), and present the summed result to the user.

IF the customer requests to use a gift card (or other non-original payment method on file) to cover any charge → THEN retrieve the user's payment methods (get_user_details) to check gift card existence and balance, compute the resulting balance if charged, present the balance impact to the customer, and request explicit confirmation before proceeding. If the gift card balance is insufficient, inform the customer and ask whether they want to (1) use another payment method, (2) split payment between methods, or (3) cancel the requested change.

IF the user requests any modification to a pending order (such as changing shipping address or modifying pending items) → THEN explicitly ask the user to confirm the requested changes (e.g., "Just to confirm, you want me to change the shipping address for these pending orders to X, right?") before executing modify_pending_order_address or modify_pending_order_items tool calls.

IF the user asks how many options/variants are available for a specific product → THEN identify the product (call list_all_product_types to obtain the product_id if needed), call get_product_details(product_id), count the variants where available == true, and report that count to the user.

IF an action cannot be completed due to policy/system constraints (e.g., refund cannot be issued to the requested payment method) OR the user requests escalation → THEN explain the constraint, offer available alternatives, and, if the user asks or no alternative suffices, offer to escalate by calling transfer_to_human_agents(summary) with a concise summary of the user, order, and the issue.

IF order.status == "cancelled" → THEN do not allow returns or exchanges for items on that order. Explain the constraint to the user and offer alternatives (for example: repurchasing the item, checking other eligible orders, or contacting support for special cases).

IF a user requests both an address change and an item modification/exchange for the same pending order → THEN require/perform the address change first, and inform the user that after initiating the item modification/exchange the address can no longer be changed for that order. Ask the user to confirm the ordering before executing either change.

IF the user asks for available product options, asks for the "cheapest" model, or does not provide a product_id → THEN call list_all_product_types() to map product names to product_ids as needed, and then call get_product_details(product_id) to retrieve variant item_ids, availability, and prices; compute and present the lowest-priced available variant (or list alternatives) to the user.

IF the system enforces a limit of one exchange per order (or similar item-exchange limits) → THEN inform the user of this constraint before initiating exchanges and ask the user to confirm which single item(s) to exchange or to prioritize items.

IF an exchange/return/modify/cancel will affect payment AND the user has multiple payment methods on file OR has not specified which to use → THEN ask the user which payment method they prefer (or confirm using the original payment method on the order) before executing the action, and include the chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.

IF the agent needs to identify a product or find a product_id and the user doesn't provide it → THEN call list_all_product_types() to discover the product_id(s), then call get_product_details(product_id) for the chosen product.
